leagueBriefing: The World Baseball Classic has reached its peak as Venezuela prepares for a historic final showdown against Team USA in Miami.

games.headline: Venezuela reaches first Classic final with dramatic seventh-inning surge past Italy
games.brief: In a night filled with South Florida rain and high-stakes drama, Venezuela punch their ticket to the World Baseball Classic final for the first time in history. Down 2-1 in the seventh, the Venezuelans ignited a two-out rally that silenced the Italian bench and sent the Miami crowd into a frenzy. It was a victory that transcended the box score, serving as a massive emotional release for a country with deep baseball roots that had never reached this stage of the tournament.
bullets: A three-run outburst in the 7th inning turned the game around, highlighted by RBI singles from Ronald Acuña Jr., Maikel Garcia, and Luis Arraez.
Eugenio Suárez provided the early spark for Venezuela with a solo home run in the 4th inning.
Angel Zerpa earned the win in relief, while Danny Palencia locked down his second save of the tournament.
Despite the loss, Italy's 'espresso-fueled' run reached its end after they held a 2-0 lead into the 4th inning.
Gleyber Torres was a perfect 2-for-2 at the plate with a walk to anchor the Venezuelan offense.
games.context: Venezuela advances to meet Team USA in the championship game, marking their first-ever appearance in a World Baseball Classic final.
